ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Collon cream-filled cylindrical biscuit rolls; closest defensible match is the generic biscuit food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E (Biscuits and crackers) is the closest defensible category for Collon; no specific code exists for cream-filled cylindrical biscuit rolls.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific snack product; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve pack of Collon contains approximately 45g, equivalent to about 8–10 individual cylindrical biscuit rolls.

nutrient_source.title: Collon (any flavour) — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Collon (any flavour), cr_id F11080011, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue (cream-filled wafer/biscuit roll) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweet biscuits and cream-filled wafer rolls — analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour biscuit/wafer category
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Collon cream-filled biscuit rolls in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food category: refined wheat flour sweet biscuits and cream wafers, which consistently score GI 65–75 in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (70) × carbohydrate per serving (45g × 70.1g/100g = 31.5g) / 100 = 22.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (41.95g/100g) promoting insulin-driven sodium retention and weight gain, despite low sodium (28mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid Collon entirely; its 41.95g/100g sugar load drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and contributes to weight gain, both of which elevate blood pressure independent of dietary sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Critically high sugar (41.95g/100g) and high carbohydrate (70.1g/100g) with low dietary fibre (2.28g/100g), producing severe postprandial glucose excursions
type2_diabetes.guidance: Collon is entirely unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; a single 45g serving delivers approximately 18.9g sugar and 31.5g carbohydrate with minimal fibre buffering, causing severe postprandial glucose spikes that are incompatible with glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.17g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~11g/100g (likely from palm oil), combined with very high sugar, promoting dyslipidaemia and atherosclerotic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Collon; its estimated 11g/100g saturated fat (likely palm oil-derived) raises LDL cholesterol, and its 41.95g/100g sugar load promotes hypertriglyceridaemia and endothelial inflammation, compounding atherosclerotic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High refined sugar (41.95g/100g) and estimated saturated fat (~11g/100g from palm oil) are strongly pro-inflammatory, promoting TNF-alpha and IL-6 cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Collon entirely; its high refined sugar and likely palm oil-derived saturated fat content are strongly pro-inflammatory and will exacerbate joint inflammation and disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Collon is a popular Japanese ultra-processed snack manufactured by Glico, consisting of small hollow cylindrical biscuit rolls made from refined wheat flour, filled with a sweet cream filling. The biscuit shell is light, crispy, and mildly sweet, while the cream interior is rich and smooth. Collon is available in multiple flavours including original cream, chocolate, strawberry, and matcha. It is sold in individual snack packs typically weighing around 45g and is widely distributed across Asia, including Singapore, where it is a common convenience-store and supermarket snack. Nutritionally, Collon is energy-dense at 503 kcal per 100g, with a very high sugar content of 41.95g/100g and total carbohydrate of 70.1g/100g, making it a high-glycaemic food. Total fat is 22.17g/100g, with estimated saturated fat of approximately 11g/100g likely derived from palm oil used in the cream filling. Dietary fibre is low at 2.28g/100g and sodium is low at 28mg/100g. Calcium and iron are reported as 0mg/100g in the HPB database. Clinically, Collon is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar, high saturated fat, and low fibre profile. It should be consumed only occasionally and in small portions by healthy individuals.
common_user_queries: Collon biscuit roll calories per pack

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080011) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous category. All mandatory sources are approved.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (11.0g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central closest analogous food (cream-filled wafer/biscuit roll, USDA FDC), as the HPB Foodo database does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry. This estimate is consistent with the known palm oil content typical of Glico Collon products. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for Collon are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 per the HPB Foodo source (confirmed zero per HPB). 4. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate based on refined wheat flour sweet biscuits and cream wafers from the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 65–75 for this category); no direct GI measurement for Collon exists in any approved source. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; Glico Collon products sold in Japan are not certified halal by default. Consumers in Singapore should verify packaging for halal certification. 6. Allergen list (gluten, milk, soy) is based on typical Collon ingredient declarations; consumers should verify current packaging.
